events.title: South Sudan risks return to full-blown civil war as violence escalates
events.description: At least 169 killed in raid near Sudan border as clashes between government and opposition forces intensify South Sudan is reeling from an escalating conflict between the government-aligned army and opposition forces and allied groups that observers say risks returning the country to a full-blown civil war. Violent confrontations in the world’s youngest country between the military, which is loyal to President Salva Kiir, and insurgents believed to be allied to the suspended vice-president, Riek Machar, have increased in recent weeks. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 South Sudan risks return to full-blown civil war as violence escalates

events.title: Vanuatu moves forward with UN climate resolution despite Trump opposition
events.description: Pacific island says the US weakened its proposal to advance a key climate ruling but vows to hold major polluters accountable The Trump administration’s attempt to sink a UN resolution demanding countries act on the climate crisis has caused cuts to the proposal but hasn’t entirely killed it, according to the tiny Pacific island country spearheading the effort. The US has demanded that Vanuatu , an archipelago in the south Pacific, drop its UN draft resolution that calls on the world to implement a landmark international court of justice (ICJ) ruling from last year that countries could face paying reparations if they fail to stem the climate crisis. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Vanuatu moves forward with UN climate resolution despite Trump opposition

events.title: ‘Imperialist undertones’: global south condemns US-Israeli war with Iran
events.description: China calls it unacceptable to ‘kill leader of sovereign state’, while South Africa questions ‘pre-emptive’ justification Middle East crisis – live updates The US-Israeli war on Iran has been condemned as illegal across much of the global south, with China saying it was unacceptable to “blatantly kill the leader of a sovereign state”. Many countries objected that negotiations between the US and Iran over its nuclear programme and missile capability were not given a chance to succeed before Washington and Israel began bombing, and analysts often saw the war in terms of a colonial-style exercise of might. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Imperialist undertones’: global south condemns US-Israeli war with Iran

events.title: Women’s rights are regressing worldwide, warns UN gender equality chief
events.description: As an increase in conflicts leads to a significant spike in gender-based violence, women across the world face a “justice gap” with discriminatory laws reported in most countries, according to a report from gender equality agency UN Women, released on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Women’s rights are regressing worldwide, warns UN gender equality chief

events.title: Austin bar shooting bodycam released as DA makes major call about cops who shot suspected terrorist
events.description: Bodycam video from the bar shooting in Austin, Texas was released Thursday as the district attorney said police officers who shot and killed the suspect will not face a grand jury.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Austin bar shooting bodycam released as DA makes major call about cops who shot suspected terrorist

events.title: Israeli army announces start of airstrikes on southern Beirut suburb
events.description: An Israeli strike targeted the southern suburbs of Beirut late Thursday, according to Lebanese state media, following an Israeli evacuation warning. Israel's military chief said Thursday he had ordered troops to expand the area under Israeli control in southern Lebanon, as the country's ongoing operation against Hezbollah intensified. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli army announces start of airstrikes on southern Beirut suburb

events.title: Iran crisis: Schoolgirls killed, thousands displaced and aid compromised
events.description: On the fourth day of Israeli and United States airstrikes against Iran and amid growing violence and instability in the Middle East, the UN urgently called for protection of civilians and warned of growing displacement and humanitarian needs.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran crisis: Schoolgirls killed, thousands displaced and aid compromised

events.title: Iran crisis: Nuclear watchdog urges restraint amid ongoing strikes
events.description: As the violent escalation in the Middle East entered a third day on Monday sparked by the bombing of Iran by Israel and the United States, the head of the UN atomic energy agency urged a return to diplomacy, given the “increasing risk to nuclear safety” in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran crisis: Nuclear watchdog urges restraint amid ongoing strikes

events.title: News live: Greens senator David Shoebridge says Australia is ‘part of an illegal war’ as PM confirms Australians on US submarine
events.description: Shoebridge says Australian personnel involvement in the Iran conflict was ‘inevitable’. Follow the latest updates live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast New Israeli ambassador to Australia says war with Iran will continue ‘as long as we need’ Israel’s new ambassador to Australia, Hillel Newman , said the conflict between the country and Iran will continue “as long as we need” to achieve multiple objectives, including removing the threat of Iranian nuclear and ballistic missile programs. The people of Iran must decide their future. We’re not deciding for them their future. It’s their choice. But we’re trying to remove the fear barrier. The people do want to change. They want to change their regime. They’re oppressed. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 News live: Greens senator David Shoebridge says Australia is ‘part of an illegal war’ as PM confirms Australians on US submarine

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Strikes continue from US, Israel and Iran as UN urges restraint
events.description: Violent escalation in the Middle East has entered a third day as coordinated US and Israeli strikes against Iran aimed at regime change continue to cause loss of life and damage across the region, prompting Iranian missile and drone counter-strikes hitting targets in multiple countries. Explosions, airspace closures and military alerts have been reported from Tehran to the Gulf states and beyond. Follow our live coverage with updates from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Strikes continue from US, Israel and Iran as UN urges restraint

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Conflict continues across region amid US, Israeli and Iranian strikes
events.description: Violence in the Middle East is continuing into a fifth day, with US and Israeli strikes against Iran and Iranian missile and drone attacks reported across several countries in the region. The escalating confrontation is disrupting airspace, transport and daily life while raising fears of a wider regional conflict. Follow our live coverage for real-time updates, reactions and humanitarian developments from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Conflict continues across region amid US, Israeli and Iranian strikes

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: UN Security Council meeting in emergency session over Iran
events.description: The UN Security Council met in emergency session in the aftermath of major airstrikes across Iran on Saturday by the United States and Israel. In retaliation, Tehran launched its own attacks throughout the Middle East. UN chief António Guterres told ambassadors the action risks “igniting a chain of events that nobody can control in the most volatile region of the world.” He added that “everything must be done to prevent further escalation.” Follow our live coverage from UN Web TV below. UN News app users can follow here and full meetings coverage can be found daily, here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: UN Security Council meeting in emergency session over Iran

events.title: DR Congo government says 200 killed after landslide at rebel-held mine
events.description: The M23, which controls the coltan-rich area, has not commented and the death toll is hard to verify.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ DR Congo government says 200 killed after landslide at rebel-held mine

events.title: US war on Iran has no Plan B, warns Scott Lucas: 'Trump just wants to kill people and blow stuff up'
events.description: As the US war on Iran escalates, sowing conflict across the Middle East, Angela Diffley is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, journalist and Professor of American Studies and International Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. The Trump administration entered this confrontation assuming that overwhelming pressure and military force would compel the Iranian regime to capitulate. Meanwhile, Israel seeks nothing short of regime change. Without a coherent plan, does Iran risk a similar fate to Iraq or Afghanistan?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US war on Iran has no Plan B, warns Scott Lucas: 'Trump just wants to kill people and blow stuff up'

events.title: Puerto Rico police say armed suspect opens ambulance door and kills injured man
events.description: Police in Puerto Rico say an armed suspect flung open the door of an ambulance and fatally shot a man who was being treated by medics for bullet wounds he received during a shooting earlier in the U.S. territory’s capital
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Puerto Rico police say armed suspect opens ambulance door and kills injured man

events.title: Azerbaijan says Iran struck its territory, as war spills over border
events.description: President Ilham Aliyev says drone strikes are "act of terror" as he places armed forces on high alert.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Azerbaijan says Iran struck its territory, as war spills over border

events.title: US Congress narrowly rejects war powers resolution to halt Trump’s Iran attacks
events.description: The US House of Representatives narrowly rejected a war powers resolution on Thursday seeking to halt President Donald Trump’s military strikes on Iran, in a 219–212 vote. The measure followed a similar Senate defeat a day earlier, highlighting divisions in Congress over Trump’s decision to bypass lawmakers and escalate the conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US Congress narrowly rejects war powers resolution to halt Trump’s Iran attacks

events.title: Iran's barrage of attacks across the Persian Gulf shows regional chaos is key to its strategy
events.description: For years, Iran’s theocratic government warned it would blanket the Middle East with missile and drone fire if it felt its existence was threatened
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's barrage of attacks across the Persian Gulf shows regional chaos is key to its strategy

events.title: Cuba charges six exiles with terrorism in wake of deadly speedboat attack
events.description: Detainees accused of coming from the US with intent to sow chaos and attack military units on Communist-ruled island Cuban prosecutors have formally charged six people with crimes of terrorism after a US-flagged speedboat was involved in a deadly shootout with Cuba’s coast guard last week. The US-based Cuban defendants are accused of packing a boat with weapons and heading toward Cuba in hopes of destabilising the government in Havana. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Cuba charges six exiles with terrorism in wake of deadly speedboat attack

events.title: North Korean leader inspects new warship, claims progress toward nuclear-armed navy
events.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected his new destroyer for two straight days ahead of its commissioning and observed a test of cruise missiles fired from the warship, vowing to accelerate the nuclear-armament of his navy, state media said Thursday
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korean leader inspects new warship, claims progress toward nuclear-armed navy

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Melania Trump presides over historic session on children in conflict
events.description: The UN Security Council convened in New York under extraordinary circumstances Monday: amid escalating conflict in the Middle East and repeated calls from the UN for restraint by all sides, the Council turns its attention to a long-standing but under-addressed crisis facing children in war zones. The United States, which holds the rotating presidency for March, decided to have First Lady Melania Trump chair the meeting. Usually, the UN ambassador or senior official takes the gavel. While there have been instances of First Ladies taking part in Security Council meetings, today was the first time a sitting First Lady has had the gavel. Follow speaker by speaker below, and UN News app users can go here. For daily in-depth meetings coverage, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Melania Trump presides over historic session on children in conflict

events.title: Attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes ‘undermine international peace and security’
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres and the heads of UN agencies have condemned Saturday’s joint Israeli and US attacks on Iran and the Iranian retaliatory strikes on Israel and the Gulf Regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes ‘undermine international peace and security’

events.title: Dismay as Hegseth urges Latin American allies to join ‘offense’ against cartels
events.description: Critics sceptical Pentagon chief’s plan for increased military force – amid rising US intervention – will stop drug gangs Pete Hegseth, the US defence secretary, has urged Latin American countries to adopt a more aggressive approach against drug cartels, warning that the Trump administration may otherwise act unilaterally in the region. Hegseth’s remarks come in a context of escalating US intervention in the region, both militarily and in elections , which culminated in the capture of Venezuelan dictator Nicolás Maduro – the first US ground military attack on a South American country. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dismay as Hegseth urges Latin American allies to join ‘offense’ against cartels

events.title: Oil price continues to rise amid Middle East crisis but stock markets rebound across Asia
events.description: Reports of attack on US registered tanker in Gulf lifts crude by 3% to $84 a barrel as gas price also starts to climb Business live – latest updates Middle East crisis – live updates Stock markets have rebounded in Asia after days of heavy losses driven by the war in the Middle East , but oil and gas prices have continued to climb amid disruption to supplies. South Korea’s KOSPI, which posted its biggest ever fall on Tuesday of 12%, rose by almost 10% on Thursday, while Japan’s Nikkei climbed by 1.9%. MSCI’s Asia-Pacific index excluding Japan jumped by 2.7%. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil price continues to rise amid Middle East crisis but stock markets rebound across Asia

events.title: Three Australians were onboard US submarine that sank Iranian warship, PM says
events.description: Anthony Albanese confirms ADF members were on vessel but says ‘no Australian personnel’ took part in attacks on Iran Middle East crisis – live updates Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese has confirmed that three Australians were on a US submarine that sank an Iranian warship , after the Labor government earlier refused to comment on reports that emerged on Thursday. The prime minister said the Australian defence force personnel were on the submarine as part of an Aukus training program. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three Australians were onboard US submarine that sank Iranian warship, PM says

events.title: Iran war: Israel launches more attacks on Tehran, Beirut
events.description: Israel has said it struck targets in Lebanon and Iran as the war in the Middle East enters its seventh day. The attacks were part of a "broad" new wave of strikes on the Iranian capital, Israel said. Follow DW for more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Israel launches more attacks on Tehran, Beirut

events.title: Israel strikes Beirut’s southern suburbs after issuing a blanket evacuation order
events.description: Israel launched a series of strikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut Thursday after ordering all residents of the densely populated area to evacuate. Traffic was gridlocked in Lebanon 's capital on Thursday as panicked residents tried to flee after Israel's military issued an evacuation notice telling residents to “save your lives and evacuate your homes immediately,” and specified which routes they should take to escape. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent reports from Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel strikes Beirut’s southern suburbs after issuing a blanket evacuation order

events.title: Israeli evacuation order for Beirut's southern suburbs sparks panic
events.description: Huge traffic jams form after the unprecedented warning for people to leave the Hezbollah stronghold, as Israel steps up its strikes across Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli evacuation order for Beirut's southern suburbs sparks panic

events.title: Imprisoned Iranian protesters face ‘expedited’ executions
events.description: As US, Israeli and Iranian strikes continued for a fifth day, a top Human Rights Council probe warned on Wednesday that Iranian prisoners including detained protesters face expedited death penalty proceedings.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Imprisoned Iranian protesters face ‘expedited’ executions

events.title: World News in Brief: Gaza crossings closed, fighting in Sudan continues, Afghanistan quake victims
events.description: The escalating violence in the Middle East region is compounding an already dire humanitarian crisis in Gaza.

events.title: Iran war: Will Southeast Asia opt for pragmatism?
events.description: Southeast Asian governments have largely struck a cautious tone after the joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran, even as the conflict widens across the Middle East and disrupts energy shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Will Southeast Asia opt for pragmatism?

events.title: How the US-Israel war with Iran is reshaping Africa
events.description: US-Israel strikes on Iran are disrupting trade, raising costs and shifting alliances. How is the escalating conflict shaping Iran's political, economic and security ties with African states, especially in the Sahel?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How the US-Israel war with Iran is reshaping Africa

events.title: 'You brought hell on yourselves': Panic and fear as Israeli rhetoric raises spectre of all-out war
events.description: The news spread like wildfire and within minutes, the streets of Beirut were gripped with panic.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 'You brought hell on yourselves': Panic and fear as Israeli rhetoric raises spectre of all-out war

events.title: GoFundMe for US soldier killed in Iran war raises more than $94K
events.description: Supporters have thus far raised more than $94,000 for the family of fallen Army Reserve Sgt. Declan Coady through a GoFundMe campaign after the soldier's death in Kuwait.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 GoFundMe for US soldier killed in Iran war raises more than $94K

events.title: $4.2M US torpedo detonates under Iranian warship in historic ‘No Mercy’ strike
events.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth calls Iranian warship sinking a message that 'gloves are off' as U.S. nuclear submarine demonstrates maritime dominance with torpedo strike.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 $4.2M US torpedo detonates under Iranian warship in historic ‘No Mercy’ strike

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Fourth day of escalating conflict between US, Israel and Iran
events.description: Violent escalation in the Middle East has entered a fourth day, with US and Israeli military strikes against Iran and ensuing Iranian counter-strikes hitting targets across the region, disrupting airspace and transport routes. The situation has caused civilian casualties and raised fears of broader instability, including in Lebanon and the Gulf. We will bring you real-time reporting and updates from across the United Nations system. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Fourth day of escalating conflict between US, Israel and Iran

events.title: Qatar says 14 ballistic missiles and 4 drones intercepted
events.description: Fresh waves of explosions, most from interceptions, also rocked Dubai, Doha and Manama on Thursday. Qatar's defence ministry said its forces intercepted 14 Iranian ballistic missiles on Thursday and 4 drones were also shot down, with no casualties reported. Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha for FRANCE 24;
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Qatar says 14 ballistic missiles and 4 drones intercepted

events.title: Iran strikes ‘squandered a chance for diplomacy’: Guterres
events.description: “Everything must be done” to prevent wider escalation of war across the Middle East following joint US-Israeli strikes against Iran, and retaliatory action from Tehran targeting multiple countries in the region, the UN Secretary-General told the Security Council on Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran strikes ‘squandered a chance for diplomacy’: Guterres

events.title: Iran war: What role will Yemen's Houthi group play?
events.description: Following the attacks on Iran by the US and Israel, the Houthi group in Yemen, who are allied with Iran, are making threats but have yet to take action.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: What role will Yemen's Houthi group play?

events.title: Morocco parts ways with coach Regragui before World Cup, Ouahbi appointed
events.description: Morocco parted ways with coach Walid Regragui on Thursday, three months before the World Cup, appointing Mohamed Ouahbi as his successor, the national federation said. Regragui departs despite leading Morocco to the 2022 World Cup semi-finals. Ouahbi, 49, previously guided the country to the Under-20 World Cup title in October.

events.title: 'We will dismantle the stronghold of Hezbollah': Israel's permanent representative to UN
events.description: Danny Danon, Israel's permanent representative to the United Nations, told FRANCE 24 that Israel "will not allow Hezbollah to attack our civilians" and "will dismantle the stronghold of Hezbollah", following an unprecedented warning to residents to evacuate all of Beirut's southern suburbs. He added that Israel has "no desire to attack anyone" but "will do whatever is necessary in order to dismantle the capabilities of Hezbollah".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'We will dismantle the stronghold of Hezbollah': Israel's permanent representative to UN

events.title: Army reservist, 20, killed in Iranian drone strike in Kuwait, spent final hours reassuring family: ‘I’m good’
events.description: The family of fallen U.S. Army Reserve Sgt. Declan Coady shared heartfelt memories after the 20-year-old Drake student was killed in an Iranian drone attack in Kuwait.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Army reservist, 20, killed in Iranian drone strike in Kuwait, spent final hours reassuring family: ‘I’m good’

events.title: California ‘party mom’ faces reckoning for hosting alcohol-fueled teen sex parties
events.description: California's "Los Gatos Party Mom" Shannon O'Connor was accused of hosting alcohol-fueled parties for minors during the COVID-19 pandemic lockdown. The jury revealed their verdict.

events.title: 74 retired US generals, admirals back Iran strikes, warn Tehran seeks to ‘spill American blood’
events.description: Seventy-four retired U.S. generals and admirals publicly endorse joint military operations with Israel targeting Iran's regional threats and capabilities.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 74 retired US generals, admirals back Iran strikes, warn Tehran seeks to ‘spill American blood’

events.title: Mine collapse in eastern Congo leaves 200 dead
events.description: In tonight's edition, at least 200 people have been killed in a coltan mine collapse in the East of the DRC. Also, unhappy with the appointment of a woman as the Archbishop of Canterbury, the Nigerian Conservative Anglican bishop group announces the creation of its own body to lead the Anglican faith. And at just 12 days until kickoff, the Confederation of African Football postpones the Women's Africa Cup of Nations until the summer.

events.title: Canada and Australia leaders urge war de-escalation, but agree Iran can't get nuclear weapons
events.description: Canadian and Australian prime ministers have called for a de-escalation of the Iran war but added the Iranians must never gain a nuclear weapon
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada and Australia leaders urge war de-escalation, but agree Iran can't get nuclear weapons

events.title: Mine collapse in eastern Congo leaves 200 dead, authorities say. But rebels dispute the number
events.description: A mine collapse at Congo’s key Rubaya coltan site has killed a number of miners

events.title: Deadly bombing of Iran primary school ‘a grave violation of humanitarian law’: UNESCO
events.description: The UN education agency, UNESCO, says that the bombing of a primary school during the US and Israeli military attacks on Iran on Saturday constitutes a grave violation of humanitarian law.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly bombing of Iran primary school ‘a grave violation of humanitarian law’: UNESCO

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Further escalation drives uncertainty and suffering
events.description: On day six of the war in the Middle East, there's been no let-up in bombs, drones and rockets targeting Iran, Israel, Lebanon and many Gulf States, while NATO forces reportedly intercepted a missile fired at Türkiye by Iran, a claim denied by Tehran. We'll bring you the latest from the United Nations and our partners at headquarters and in the field. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE: Further escalation drives uncertainty and suffering

events.title: Iran’s drone swarm attacks unleash ‘exponential costs’ on US, prolonging war: 'Asymmetric capability'
events.description: Iranian drones reportedly struck U.S. Embassy in Riyadh and Dubai consulate as defense expert Cameron Chell warns Tehran coordinates mass UAV attacks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s drone swarm attacks unleash ‘exponential costs’ on US, prolonging war: 'Asymmetric capability'

events.title: Trump urges Iranian diplomats around the world to request asylum
events.description: Trump touted the U.S. ​military actions in Iran on Thursday, saying they were ‌destroying Tehran's missile and drone capability and that "their navy is gone - 24 ships in three days," as ‌he called on Iranian diplomats to request asylum and help shape ​a better country. "We also urge Iranian diplomats around the world to request asylum and to help us shape a ​new and better Iran," he said. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump urges Iranian diplomats around the world to request asylum

events.title: Trump unlikely to ever commit ground US forces to Iran, expert says
events.description: Top US officials, including the Defense Secretary, have said that sending US troops into Iran could not be ruled out. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Daniel Schneiderman, Director of Global Policy Programs at Penn Washington. He says that US President Donald Trump is unlikely to ever commit ground forces to Iran because "there's a degree of ability to deny that the US is involved in a long-standing war during an air campaign".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump unlikely to ever commit ground US forces to Iran, expert says

events.title: These women fought in Ethiopia's last civil war and warn against another one
events.description: Female fighters recall how the conflict in the northern Tigray destroyed their lives, as fears grow that fighting could resume.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 These women fought in Ethiopia's last civil war and warn against another one

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war live: Trump says Iran being ‘demolished’ as Gulf attacks continue

events.title: Why has Hezbollah joined Middle East war?
events.description: Hezbollah has been attacking Israel as it pummels southern Lebanon and parts of Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why has Hezbollah joined Middle East war?

events.title: Canadian backpacker Piper James died ‘as a result of drowning’ following dingo attack at K’gari, coroner says
events.description: Cause of death of 19-year-old in January was drowning ‘in the setting of multiple injuries, due to, or as a consequence of a dingo attack’ Canadian backpacker Piper James died “as a result of drowning” following a dingo attack at K’gari, the Queensland coroners court says. The court on Friday said Piper’s cause of death had been determined by a forensic pathologist and accepted by the investigating coroner. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Canadian backpacker Piper James died ‘as a result of drowning’ following dingo attack at K’gari, coroner says

events.title: Morning news brief
events.description: Middle East conflict enters Day 6, clerics convene to replace Iran's supreme leader, China to send special envoy to help mediate the US-Israel-Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Morning news brief

events.title: Oscar-nominated Iranian dissident warns against 'repeat of the past'
events.description: Mehdi Mahmoudian worries Iran could experience an endless cycle of violence unless — or until — it achieves democracy.

events.title: Iran war strains US-Spain relations

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war strains US-Spain relations

events.title: No, Iranian footballer Mehdi Taremi is not going to fight for the Iranian regime
events.description: The claim spread like wildfire on social media earlier this week after the US and Israel began striking Iran: Iranian footballer Mehdi Taremi was considering leaving Olympiakos, where he currently plays, to go back to his homeland to fight for the Iranian regime. The unfounded rumour was then picked up by reputable sports press, particularly in Italy, where Taremi used to play for Inter Milan.

events.title: US claims Iran's missile capabilities 'severely degraded'
events.description: Washington claims it has severely degraded Iran's missile capabilities, but as the war continues and Iran retaliates, it seems that the country's military capabilities are far from being extinguished. FRANCE 24's Luke Shrago reports.

events.title: Ukraine peace talks could be postponed for 'a while' due to Iran war

events.title: Heart-stopping video shows bunk bed collapse nearly impale little boy, sister’s heroic split-second reaction
events.description: A dramatic bunk bed collapse caught on camera showed a sister attempting to rescue her trapped brother after the bed's metal frame failed. The viral video gained more than 82 million views.

events.title: Private flights account for 30% of departures from Oman airport as wealthy evacuate Middle East
events.description: Private flights account for more than 30% of departures from Oman's main airport as Middle East evacuation efforts intensify amid conflict escalation.

events.title: 'Quiet Death': What to know about the American torpedo that sank Iranian warship, killing 87

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Freed Argentine officer urges release of remaining foreign prisoners in Venezuela
events.description: Nahuel Gallo, an Argentine military police officer, is urging the international community to push Venezuela to free 24 foreigners still in detention

events.title: The future of war? US-Israel blitz on Iran unveils next-gen allied combat

events.title: Carney says he backs strikes on Iran 'with some regret' as world order frays

events.title: Families of Kenyan recruits duped into fighting in Russia's war demand sons return

events.title: Iran continues firing missiles, drones at neighboring states, with multiple interceptions reported

Images of the seven-month-old dragging around a toy bigger than him drew attention to the residents of Ichikawa city zoo near Tokyo. When other monkeys shooed the baby away, Punch rushed back to the toy orangutan, hugging it for comfort. Continue reading...

On the eve of the new Formula One season in Melbourne, the seven-time champion outlined his ambition to compete in a grand prix on African soil. But the 41-year-old, F1’s first black race driver, did not stop there. He suggested former colonial rulers still exerted undue power in the region and called for action to reverse that influence. “I’ve got roots from a few different places there, like Togo and Benin,” he said. “I’m really proud of that part of the world. events.title: Three men deported by US file legal case against Eswatini over detention
events.description: The men, sent to Africa after completing criminal sentences in the US, are from Cuba, Jamaica and Yemen Three men deported by the US to Eswatini – rather than their home countries – have filed a case against Eswatini’s government with the African Union’s human rights body, claiming their detention was an unlawful violation of their rights. Two of the claimants, from Cuba and Yemen, have been in prison in Eswatini, formerly Swaziland, for eight months . The third, Orville Etoria, was repatriated to his home country , Jamaica, in September. Continue reading...

events.title: US military launches operation in Ecuador to combat drug trafficking

Southern Command, which encompasses 31 countries through South and Central America and the Caribbean, said in a statement on X that the “decisive action” was aimed at combating illicit drug trafficking. events.title: China sets lowest GDP growth target for decades as it braces for economic slowdown
events.description: ‘High-quality growth’ target of 4.5-5% outlined at Two Sessions as Chinese premier talks of complex situations at home and abroad What are China’s Two Sessions and why do they matter? China has set its target for GDP growth to a record low of 4.5-5%, the first time since 1991 that the figure has dropped below 5%, reflecting an economic strategy that is shifting away from export-led growth to a model that leaders hope will be more resilient to external shocks. Li Qiang, China’s premier, announced the target for 2026 in the opening session of the National People’s Congress (NPC), China’s annual parliamentary gathering, which began on Thursday. Continue reading...

events.title: Why does Yao Ming, the 7ft 6in ex-basketballer, attend China’s Two Sessions meetings?
events.description: Among the 3,000 delegates is former athlete who sits as an independent on the National People’s Congress What are China’s Two Sessions and why do they matter? More generals purged as gather in Beijing Among the generally drab lineup of mostly middle-aged men in suits who make up the nearly 3,000 delegates to the National People’s Congress (NPC), China’s parliament, a few stand out. There are delegates from China’s 55 official ethnic minority groups, who often arrive dressed in traditional outfits rather than western-style suits. There are military members, identifiable by their uniforms. And then there is Yao Ming, the 7ft and 6in tall retired basketball player who, towering over every other person in the Great Hall of the People, is hard to miss. Continue reading...

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: South Africa’s president calls Trump’s policy to offer refuge to white Afrikaners ‘racist’
events.description: US president is ‘truly uninformed’ for spreading claims of ‘white genocide’ in South Africa, Cyril Ramaphosa tells New York Times South Africa’s president, Cyril Ramaphosa, has called Donald Trump’s policy of allowing white Afrikaners to apply for refugee status in the US “racist”, saying the US president was “truly uninformed” in a rare instance of direct criticism. Ramaphosa told the New York Times that last year’s Oval Office meeting with the US leader, when Trump turned down the lights and played a video that he falsely claimed showed there was a “white genocide” in South Africa, was a “spectacle” and an “ambush”. Continue reading...

events.title: China sets a lower economic growth target of 4.5% to 5% for 2026 as challenges loom
events.description: China has signaled continuity rather than change for its economy, setting a slightly lower target for growth this year in the midst of a property slump and other headwinds at home and growing uncertainty abroad.

events.title: Kristi Noem to be replaced as DHS secretary, Trump says
events.description: Trump has said he wants Oklahoma Senator Markwayne Mullin to serve as the next head of the Department of Homeland Security, replacing embattled Kristi Noem.

events.title: How debt deals are funding the fight against ecosystem collapse
events.description: Debt-for-nature swaps and conservation funds to halt biodiversity loss are gaining traction as governments link ecosystem collapse to geopolitical instability.

events.title: Football: Women's AFCON postponed at 11th hour
events.description: After weeks of uncertainty, football's governing body in Africa has announced the postponement of the Women's Africa Cup of Nations, which had been slated to start in mid-March.

events.title: Cologne Cathedral to start charging admission fee
events.description: The iconic cathedral's chapter said the fee is meant to cover the increasing cost of the facility's maintenance, security and operation. The cathedral attracts some 6.6 million visitors annually.

events.title: Paralympic Winter Games: What you need to know
events.description: Just weeks after hosting the Winter Olympics, northern Italy is set to host the Winter Paralympic Games. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

events.title: Why all the hype for 'The Devil Wears Prada 2'?
events.description: Fans are eagerly awaiting the sequel ― and the return of those iconic characters and their vicious power plays. But "The Devil Wears Prada 2" also shows us just how much fashion and media have changed since 2006.

events.title: Trump says Zelensky ‘has to get on the ball’ to reach Russia deal
events.description: US President Donald Trump on Thursday said Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky was the main obstacle to reaching a deal with Russia, describing President Vladimir Putin as cooperative. Trump said Zelensky “has to get on the ball” and make compromises to secure an agreement.

events.title: Is China training an army of robots? Nope, it’s an AI-generated video
events.description: Some media users have been sharing videos that they claim are proof that China has started training humanoid robot soldiers. It turns out, however, that these videos were – surprise surprise – created by AI.

events.title: The Royal Navy's destroyer will arrive too late for Cyprus - the French are already here
events.description: Twice, the air raid sirens sounded around RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus this afternoon. We were rushed inside as soldiers lay on the floor in body armour. It was over quickly.

events.title: Cologne Cathedral, one of Germany's best-known landmarks, to charge tourists for admission
events.description: Cologne Cathedral, a famous German landmark and popular tourist draw, will start charging an admission fee to visitors as church officials struggle with rising maintenance costs

events.title: A Syrian man is convicted of stabbing a Spanish tourist at Berlin's Holocaust Memorial
events.description: A court has convicted a Syrian man of stabbing and seriously wounding a Spanish tourist at Berlin’s Holocaust Memorial just over a year ago

events.title: Czech lawmakers will vote on lifting immunity for populist leader Babiš over EU fraud case
events.description: Czech lawmakers are set to consider lifting former Prime Minister Andrej Babiš’ immunity so prosecutors can press a fraud case

events.title: Millions left without power after major blackout hits Cuba's western region
events.description: A blackout has left millions of people without power in Havana and the rest of western Cuba in the latest outage on an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electric grid

events.title: Claims of 'rediscovered' Michelangelos unsettle Renaissance experts
events.description: An independent researcher has asserted that a marble bust of Christ in a Roman church is by Michelangelo

events.title: 2 men on trial on charges they spied on Hong Kong diaspora in the UK

events.title: ‘AI Kid of India’ urges young people to embrace technology
events.description: 16-year-old Raul John Aju – dubbed the “AI Kid of India” at home – is a business prodigy who advises government and industry and has created several innovative AI tools.

events.title: Ohio mother charged with murdering two daughters found buried in suitcases near Cleveland
events.description: An Ohio mother was charged with aggravated murder after her two young daughters were found buried in separate suitcases in shallow graves in Cleveland earlier this week, officials said.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ohio mother charged with murdering two daughters found buried in suitcases near Cleveland

events.title: Former New York DARE officer admits to selling drugs while in uniform in squad car
events.description: A former DARE officer admitted to selling drugs while on duty in a squad car. Michael Tapscott pleaded guilty to 40 charges after a 13-year police career.

events.title: Georgia sheriff arrested on DUI charge after blood alcohol content was allegedly almost triple legal limit
events.description: A Georgia sheriff was arrested for allegedly driving a county vehicle with a blood alcohol level nearly three times the legal limit late last month.

events.title: US government contractor accused of stealing $46M arrested on small island, FBI announces
events.description: The FBI and French authorities arrested a former government contractor accused of stealing $46 million in cryptocurrency from the U.S. Marshals Service.

events.title: NYC Dept of Education employee arrested, charged with murder of Bronx father
events.description: NYC education worker Naya Brown has been charged in connection with the killing of a Bronx father as he celebrated his birthday in January.

events.title: US-Israel blitz cripples Iran as uncontested airspace nears and more top headlines

events.title: Nancy Guthrie's abductor may have returned to the crime scene, left critical clues at tribute: expert

events.title: Held at gunpoint at 9, Iranian refugee turned pastor now prays for Iran’s hour of freedom
events.description: Pastor David Nasser described his family's harrowing 1979 escape from Iran, and he expressed hope and prayers for democracy for Iranians.

events.title: Iran postpones Tehran farewell ceremony for Khamenei where large crowds were expected to gather
events.description: Iran has delayed a farewell ceremony for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei following his assassination, citing crowd size and infrastructure challenges.
